canto_title: The Tenth Bolgia: The Alchemists and the Italic Souls

scenes.summary: Virgil tells Dante to stop focusing on the weeping shadows and to look ahead, as time is short and much remains to be seen.
Dante explains his distraction, and Virgil reveals he was watching a specific soul from his own bloodline being punished.
Virgil warns Dante not to dwell on the fallen, while Dante reflects on the pride and lack of vengeance surrounding the man's death.
As the travelers reach the edge of the final ditch of Malebolge, they are met with a terrifying stench of decay.
Dante describes a landscape so filled with malice that the very air becomes poisonous, killing all living creatures.
The travelers observe the exhausted and suffering souls who are unable to even move themselves in the dark path.
Dante witnesses two souls desperately clawing at their skin due to the unbearable torment of scabies.
Virgil addresses the scratching souls to see if any Latins are present and identifies himself as a guide through Hell.
The souls turn to Dante, who asks them to identify themselves and their origins despite their agonizing state.
An inhabitant of Arezzo explains how his prideful boast about flight led to his death by fire and his punishment via alchemy.
Dante makes a witty, disparaging remark comparing the vanity of the Arezzans to that of the Sienese.
A second soul, Capocchio, identifies himself and his companions from Siena before revealing his own identity as a fraudster.
